sourceMovie.title: The Raid
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Raid for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a documentary rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kevin Michael Johnson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: American Pie: Revealed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Raid clicked for you, American Pie: Revealed (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in documentary, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: McQueen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Raid clicked for you, McQueen (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in documentary, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Raid?
faq.answer: Try American Pie: Revealed, McQueen, No Half Measures: Creating the Final Season of Breaking Bad—each shares DNA with The Raid while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Raid?
faq.answer: The Raid (2012) is best described as Documentary. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Raid worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this documentary lane.
